name: Basmati Rice with Mushrooms, Broccoli and Oni
recipeIngredient: 40 Pearl onions, peeled and ends removed
2 cups Basmati rice (or substitute long grained brown rice)
2 cups Mushrooms, wild or domestic - sliced
2 cups Rapini or broccoli florets, blanched in boiling water
recipeInstructions:
Note: Can be made vegan by omitting the Parmesan cheese.
1. Moisten a large nonstick saucepan with 1 tablespoon olive oil, then heat over medium-high heat.
2. Add the onions, cooking until tender and browned on both sides, about 7 minutes.
3. Add the garlic, cooking it until it begins to brown, about 2 minutes.
4. Add the rice, heating until it is toasted and hot.
5. Add the paprika and mix to combine well.
6. Add the white wine and cook until all the liquid is gone, about 3 minutes.
7. Add the hot vegetable stock. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Cover with a tight-fitting lid and lower heat to a simmer.
9. Cook until just tender, about 15-25 minutes, depending upon your choice of rice.
10. Meanwhile, in a large nonstick skillet over high heat, add the remaining 1 tablespoon oil.
11. Add the mushrooms, cooking them until golden, about 5 minutes.
12. Add the rapini or broccoli, cooking until al dente, about 3 minutes.
13. Transfer the mushrooms and broccoli to the saucepan with the rice.
14. Season with salt and pepper.
15. Add 1/4 cup chives.
16. Cover pan, remove from heat, and allow to sit for 5 minutes before serving.
17. Serve in warm serving plates and sprinkle with remaining chives and Parmesan cheese if desired.
